title: National Bank of Egypt (NBE) (Egypt) Profile & Reviews
description: Explore a detailed profile and institutional data for National Bank of Egypt (NBE) in Egypt. Share your experience or access institutional-grade data for professional insights.

faq.question: What is National Bank of Egypt (NBE)?
faq.answer: National Bank of Egypt (NBE) is Egypt's largest and oldest commercial bank, founded in 1898. As a state-owned universal bank, NBE plays a pivotal role in the Egyptian economy, serving approximately 17.8 million customers through its extensive network of over 615 branches nationwide. NBE holds the dominant position in Egypt's banking sector, accounting for approximately 25% of total banking system assets, 27.5% of total deposits, and commanding 74% of the credit card market and 40% of the debit card market as of recent years.

faq.question: When was National Bank of Egypt (NBE) founded?
faq.answer: National Bank of Egypt was established on June 25, 1898, with an initial capital of £1 million. The bank was founded through a partnership between Sir Ernest Cassel (50% ownership), the Suares brothers (Raphael, Joseph, and Felix), Moise Cattaui (25%), and Constantine Salvagos of Alexandria (25%). Originally established to finance Egypt's growing cotton industry and provide financial services to the government, NBE received exclusive rights to issue banknotes starting April 3, 1899. Throughout its history, NBE's functions evolved significantly: during the 1950s, it assumed central bank duties; following nationalization in 1960, the Egyptian government created a separate central bank, and NBE transitioned to operate as a pure commercial bank while still carrying out central banking functions in areas where the Central Bank had no presence.

faq.question: Where is National Bank of Egypt (NBE) headquartered?
faq.answer: NBE is headquartered in Cairo, Egypt, with its main office located at 1187 Corniche El Nil, Ramlet Beaulac Area. The bank's iconic headquarters building has been a fixture of Cairo's financial district since the early 20th century. NBE maintains significant international presence through its subsidiary in London (National Bank of Egypt UK Limited), branches in New York and Shanghai, and representative offices in Johannesburg, Dubai, and Addis Ababa.

faq.question: Where does National Bank of Egypt (NBE) operate?
faq.answer: NBE operates extensively throughout Egypt, with a comprehensive network of over 615 branches, offices, and banking units spread across all Egyptian governorates. The bank maintains the largest branch network in the country, serving both urban centers like Cairo, Alexandria, and major cities, as well as rural and remote areas. The bank's domestic infrastructure includes over 2,150 ATMs strategically deployed nationwide, representing approximately one-third of Egypt's total ATM market. NBE also operates 11,000+ point-of-sale (POS) terminals across the country, making it the leading provider of electronic payment infrastructure in Egypt.

faq.question: What services does National Bank of Egypt (NBE) offer?
faq.answer: NBE provides a comprehensive suite of banking services across retail, corporate, and investment banking segments: Retail Banking: Personal accounts, savings certificates, investment certificates, time deposits, mutual funds, consumer loans, credit cards, debit cards, and prepaid cards. Corporate Banking: Large corporate financing, SME lending (with a portfolio exceeding EGP 13.5 billion serving over 37,000 SME customers), trade finance, treasury services, payroll services for over 890 government entities, and project financing for key sectors including oil, power, telecommunications, tourism, and contracting. Digital Services: Al Ahly Net online banking platform, NBE Mobile application (with over 5 million downloads), Al Ahly App smartphone application, chatbot customer service, Phone Cash mobile payment service, and Al Ahly E-Shopping e-commerce platform. NBE commands over 50% of e-payment transactions in Egypt and is pioneering AI-powered "Digital Human" virtual assistants for customer service. Specialized Services: Investment banking through securities trading and central depositary services, microfinance programs, agricultural and industrial project financing, government bond and T-bill investments, and correspondent banking services with a global network centered in Europe.

faq.question: What makes National Bank of Egypt (NBE) unique?
faq.answer: NBE stands out as Egypt's first and oldest bank with over 125 years of continuous operation, deeply intertwined with the country's economic development. The bank has played historical roles beyond traditional banking, including issuing currency, serving as a semi-official central bank, and managing investment certificates on behalf of the Egyptian government since the mid-1960s. NBE is a leader in digital banking innovation in the Middle East and North Africa region. In 2024, NBE became the first bank in MENA to integrate AI-powered Digital Humans into its banking operations, partnering with Mastercard to revolutionize customer service delivery. The bank pioneered financial inclusion initiatives for underserved populations, including launching Egypt's first "Touch Card" for visually impaired customers in partnership with Mastercard, providing 28 ATM buses with special escalators for mobility-impaired customers, and offering 60 finance programs under the "Himma" initiative for people with disabilities. NBE maintains unmatched market dominance, controlling nearly one-quarter of Egypt's entire banking system and serving as the primary financier of major national infrastructure projects and strategic economic sectors.

faq.question: What type of financial institution is National Bank of Egypt (NBE)?
faq.answer: National Bank of Egypt is a universal bank and state-owned enterprise. As a universal bank, NBE provides the full spectrum of financial services including retail banking, commercial banking, corporate banking, investment banking, and treasury operations. The bank is wholly owned by the Egyptian government and is listed on the Egyptian Exchange (EGC). NBE functions as a systemically important financial institution in Egypt, with its creditworthiness closely correlated to Egyptian sovereign ratings by international agencies like Fitch and Moody's. As the country's largest bank by assets, deposits, loans, branches, and employees (approximately 18,500 staff), NBE serves as a strategic partner to the government in implementing national economic policies, financing developmental projects, and supporting financial inclusion and digital transformation initiatives aligned with Egypt Vision 2030.
